qoq_changes: Revenue grew to $7.69 billion from an implied $7.44 billion in Q1 2025
Net income grew to $872 million from an implied $709 million in Q1 2025
Assets held for sale surged to $4.33 billion from $0 at the end of FY2024
new_risks: GAAP operating margins turned negative (-$134 million) due to heavy R&D and acquisition-related amortization
Heavy reliance on an $834 million tax benefit to achieve positive net income for the quarter
Uncertainty surrounding a major new divestiture or restructuring, evidenced by $4.33 billion in assets held for sale
management_tone: Cautiously bullish; while top-line growth is exceptionally strong at 32% YoY, core operating profitability is masked by heavy acquisition amortization and a massive one-time tax benefit.
analyst_note: AMD delivered strong Q2 2025 revenue of $7.69B (+32% YoY), but the $872M net income was entirely driven by an $834M tax benefit and discontinued operations. Core GAAP operating income swung to a $134M loss due to $1.89B in R&D and $568M in amortization, while the balance sheet reveals a major impending divestiture with $4.3B in assets held for sale.
